name: paper-lookup
tagline: Searches 10 academic literature APIs (PubMed, arXiv, Semantic Scholar, Crossref, etc.) and returns papers/citations with reproducible proven
verdict: Well-scoped and defensively written: it explicitly warns against following instructions embedded in fetched results, never echoing API keys, and bounds/rate-limits calls before asking to proceed. Minor gaps: no explicit 'don't use for X' guidance, and it instructs using WebFetch as the primary fetch tool without declaring WebFetch in allowed-tools (only Read

highlights.summary: No explicit 'do not use for X' guidance in the description or body; only implicit scope discipline ('Don't fan out across all ten just because they're available', line 21). Minor gap, low risk given t

allowed-tools declares only 'Read Bash', but the 'Making API Calls' section (lines 104-113) instructs using WebFetch as the primary HTTP fetch tool for Claude Code, with curl/Bash as fallback — WebFet
